scope_note: QA Equilateral Triangle Series cert [319]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1993) Quantum Arithmetic Book 2 (QA-2), ITAM Portland, ISBN 1-883401-07-0, Chapter 7 pp.99-117. Companion cert [152] covers W/Y/Z definitions, Eisenstein norms, F+Y=W. This cert covers the SERIES structure. CLAIM: (C1) b=1 table (e=1..10) exact match against Iverson QA-2 p.106 (W,F,Y,Z for 10 entries). (C2) Fractal nesting F(b+1)=W(b) for e=1 series b=1..24; closed form W(b)=(b+1)(b+3); Iverson 'the triangles neatly fit together in fractal style' p.107. (C3) Every-third-3: gcd(W,F,Y,Z) divisible by 3 iff 3|b for e=1 series b=1..24; Iverson 'every third triangle has a common factor of 3' p.107. (C4) F=ab is identical in (i) equilateral-triangle base segment and (ii) Pythagorean odd-leg; verified {1..9}^2; Iverson synthesis: 'The value of F is the same for these triangles; for the height of the right triangles, and for the Latus Rectum of the ellipses' p.100. (C5) Theorem NT: closed form W(b)=(b+1)(b+3) is exact integer (no irrationals); geometric figures are observer projections; W,F,Y,Z integer values are QA claims; Iverson: 'This Book completes the BASIC part of Quantum Arithmetic' p.114. Builds on cert [152] (equilateral W/Y/Z/Eisenstein), cert [310] (Pythagorean squaring map, F=ba).
